title: Gujarati Vowel Sounds
description: Practice all 13 Gujarati vowels with IPA, mouth position guides, and minimal pair contrasts.

blocks.title: The 13 Vowels — Tap Each to Practice
points: અ (a) — like 'u' in 'cup' · Example: અહીં (ahee) = Here
આ (aa) — like 'a' in 'father' · Example: આવો (aavo) = Come
ઇ (i) — like 'i' in 'bit' · Example: ઇચ્છા (ichchhaa) = Wish
ઈ (ee) — like 'ee' in 'feet' · Example: ઈંટ (eent) = Brick
ઉ (u) — like 'u' in 'put' · Example: ઉઠો (utho) = Get up
ઊ (oo) — like 'oo' in 'food' · Example: ઊન (oon) = Wool
એ (e) — like 'e' in 'café' · Example: એક (ek) = One
ઓ (o) — like 'o' in 'note' · Example: ઓઢ (odh) = Cover
ઔ (au) — like 'ou' in 'out' · Example: ઔષધ (aushadh) = Medicine

blocks.body: 🎯 Focus on SHORT vs LONG vowels: અ vs આ, ઇ vs ઈ, ઉ vs ઊ. The LENGTH CHANGES THE MEANING. Example: 'kal' can mean yesterday OR tomorrow depending on context — length matters!

blocks.title: Critical Vowel Contrasts
blocks.instruction: These pairs differ only in vowel LENGTH. Train your ear to hear the difference!

blocks.title: Vowel Sequence Practice
blocks.text: અ આ ઇ ઈ ઉ ઊ — ક કા કિ કી કુ કૂ
blocks.romanization: a aa i ee u oo — ka kaa ki kee ku koo
blocks.translation: All vowels, then the same vowels attached to consonant 'K'
blocks.tip: Start very slowly. Each vowel-pair practice trains your ear for short vs long distinction.

items.explanation: Yes! Gujarati has 13 vowels: અ આ ઇ ઈ ઉ ઊ ઋ એ ઐ ઓ ઔ અં અઃ

items.statement: Short and long vowels mean the same thing in Gujarati.
items.explanation: No! Length is meaningful. The word 'kal' can mean yesterday or tomorrow — context and length matter!

items.statement: Aspirated consonants (like ખ, ઘ) are produced with a puff of air.
items.explanation: Correct! Hold your hand in front of your mouth — you should feel a puff of air when saying ખ (kh) but not ક (k).
